learning_outcomes:
1 1.  |  Major Programme:
The expected learning
outcomes of the Biology Programme:
Knowledge outcomes:
(1)  |  Basic knowledge in biology
(2)  |  In-depth understanding in at
least one major area of biology
Skills outcomes:
(1)  |  Skills in scientific writing, reading comprehension
and oral communication
(2)  |  Ability to work independently and in a team
(3)  |  Ability to use basic statistics and computer skills to
process and analyze data scientifically
(4)  |  Competence in using information technologies
(5)  |  Ability to use scientific method in problem solving
Values and attitude
outcomes:
(1)  |  Appreciation of biological knowledge
(2)  |  Sense of biological conservation and other environmental issues
(3)  |  Awareness on the latest developments and advancements in biology
2.  |  Biology and Chemistry Double Major Programme:
Upon completion
of the programme, students will have acquired the abilities as follows:
Knowledge outcomes: To train our students to have fundamental and advanced knowledge
of modern biological sciences and the ability of applying the acquired
knowledge in exploring chemistry-related problems. Specifically, they
include:
(1)  |  Basic
knowledge in biology, including diversity and functioning of various living
organisms
(2)  |  In‐depth
understanding in at least one major area of biology, such as physiology,
ecology and evolution, and human biology
(3)  |  Chemistry
principles, concepts, and applications of inorganic chemistry, organic
chemistry, physical chemistry, and analytical chemistry
(4)  |  Mathematical
techniques required in chemical science
Skills
outcomes: With
our team of teaching staff coming from different professional backgrounds and
with teaching styles, students will be exposed to various learning activities
and challenges throughout their 4 years of study, during which they can
develop professional skills and generic attributes including active
learning, self-learning, effective communication, critical thinking, team
playing, as well as analytical and problem-solving skills. Specifically, they include:
(1)  |  Basic
laboratory/field techniques in at least one major line of modern biological
research, including molecular biology, physiology and field biology
(2)  |  Ability
of recognizing key factors and applying appropriate assumption to formulate
solutions for chemistry problems
(3)  |  Basic
mathematical skills for handling experimental data and quantitative arguments
in physical chemistry
(4)  |  Ability
of determining molecular structure based on spectroscopic measurements
(5)  |  Ability
to implement safety measures in conducting chemical laboratory works
(6)  |  Ability
to operate instruments in chemistry laboratories
(7)  |  Ability
in carrying out prescribed procedures using suited experimental skills and
instrumentation
(8)  |  Ability
to conduct literature reviews/experimental works and to report scientific findings
in both oral and written formats
(9)  |  Competence
in using commercial computer software for graphical depiction of molecular
systems
(10)  |  Problem
solving skills including ability to identify key issues and formulate
feasible solutions of problems based on logical analysis and critical
thinking
(11)  |  Ability
to sort out complex observations to construct logical proposition
(12)  |  Ability
of formulating logical sequence in arguments
(13)  |  Critical
evaluation of issues based on evidence and scientific logic
(14)  |  Ability
of analysing issues quantitatively with the sense of level of uncertainty and
reliability
(15)  |  Ability
to acquire new knowledge by self-study and to conduct independent work with
logical planning
(16)  |  Interpersonal
skills including the ability to work effectively in a group
(17)  |  Information
retrieval skills through library and internet
(18)  |  Time
management skills
Values and attitude outcomes: V
= We
also concern about the well-being of our students. Apart from the long-established
academic advisory system, there are ample chances for teachers and students
to meet and exchange ideas formally and informally. These activities,
organised by the programme, Biology Society, Chemistry Society or Alumni
Association of the Department of Biology, The Chinese University of Hong Kong
(CUBIOAA), include tea parties, dinners, BBQ, and career talks. From these,
we believe our students are primed with positive values, and will be open and
respectful to different views or ideas, so as to act as positive contributors
in our society, no matter what fields and positions they are in. Our students
also have the ability to appreciate the nature, and diversity of life and
thus, would contributing to preserving our mother Earth. Specifically, they
include:
(1)  |  Appreciation of
biological knowledge and the beauty of chemistry and their importance as
central science
(2)  |  Sense of
biological conservation and other environmental issues
(3)  |  Awareness on the
latest developments and advancements in biology
(4)  |  Awareness of
impacts of chemistry in environmental, social, economic, industrial, and
technological aspects
(5)  |  Awareness of
chemical safety
(6)  |  Curiosity and
interest in scientific exploration
(7)  |  Confidence in
ability of solving problems
(8)  |  Motivation and
ability for life-long learning
(9)  |  Sense of taking
responsibility in study and work environments
3.  |  Minor
Programme:
Upon the
completion of the Minor Programme, students will have a basic understanding
of fundamental issues related to modern biology and acquired basic knowledge
of cell biology, genetics and ecology to allow further study in subjects such
as biotechnology and environmental sciences or to pursue a related career.

Biology Biology Applicable
to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 64[a] units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Group A: LSCI1002 Group B: CHEM1280 (preferred) or 1070 A course from the following
Group C: MATH1520 (preferred) or 1010 or 1018 Group D: (PHYS1001 or 1002) (preferred) or 1111 Group E: STAT1012 (preferred) or 1011
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  30
BCHE2030, BIOL2120, 2210, 2410, 2420, 3012, 3022, 3560,
3570, 3610, 3620, 4010, LSCI1012
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  25
(a)  |  One laboratory course from BIOL2213 or 2313 and
another laboratory course from BIOL4012 or MBTE4033#
(b)  |  BCHE3050#, 3080#, 4040#, 4060#, 4090#, 4130#,
BIOL3310, 3410, 3510, 3530, 3630 (or FNSC4101# and 4102#), 3710, 4120, 4230,
4240, 4242, 4260, 4310, 4420, 4510, 4610, CMBI4101#, 4102#, 4201#, 4202#,
4203#, FNSC3010#, 3180#, LSCI2002#, 2003#, 3012#, 3030#, 3333#, 3520# (or
ENSC3520#), MBTE3510#, 3511#, 3518#, 3521#, 3528#, 3531#, 3550#, 3558#,
3560#, 3568#, 4320#, 4510#, 4530#, STAT3210# and any course not taken in 3(a)
(c)  |  Capstone
course (at least 3 units, with a maximum of 7 units): BIOL4901,
4902, 4903, 4906, 4907, LSCI4000#, 4911#, 4912#, 4913#
Total:  |  64[a]
In addition to fulfilling
the above Major Programme Requirement, students meeting the criteria as
specified by the Faculty can take the following stream offered by the
Faculty:
Science, Technology And
Research Stream
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the two remaining groups of the Faculty
Package that have not been used to fulfill the Faculty Package
Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[b]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive
weeks of outside Hong Kong exposure[c]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  BIOL courses at 2000 and
above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in the
calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Study Option: Berkeley
Biosciences Study Abroad Programme Students who undergo one
term of studies at the University of California, Berkeley, USA will take
courses offered by the Berkeley Biosciences Study Abroad Programme. Units
obtained from the Programme will be recognized as up to 12 units of Major
Programme requirement. This option will be recorded on transcript.
[b]  |  Students may select
research-oriented course(s), as approved by the Major Programme, to
substitute up to 4 units for fulfillment of Research Courses requirement.
[c]  |  Students must complete
any exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement.
Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the exchange/research/internship
programme(s).
